text:
Once upon a time in a cozy little village, there lived a girl named Lily. She loved flowers the most. Every spring you'd find her wandering through fields wildflowers.

One particular spring she stumbled upon an old book. It wasn't anything special—she didn't read often—but there was one picture that paused her heart.

There were delicate wildflowers, just like her favorites.

The book spoke of those wildflowers. It told how a wise old owl would coax them into dazzling displays just once a year.

"You just wait," the owl Said. "You'll bloom."

And something within Lily felt that warm tickle.

Each dawn she'd walk those fields. She'd pick a petal.

There was a purple so deep.

That fall she planted some seeds.

They grew.

They exploded.

And now those blooms hang year-round. At dusk you could listen to them sing.

They were her little treasures—a secret place. You wouldn't know her there—not so loud.
